skill.name: firebase-basics
skill.description: The definitive, foundational skill for ANY Firebase task. Covers initial setup, core principles, workflows, service setup, Firebase CLI commands, troubleshooting, and environment updates.
skill.summary: Complete Firebase development skill for AI agents covering setup, usage, and maintenance.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 298 patterns but all are false positives. The skill contains documentation for legitimate Firebase CLI usage and standard development setup. External command patterns reference official npx firebase-tools commands. Network references link to official documentation. Filesystem references describe standard configuration files for development environments. No malicious intent or dangerous patterns detected.

content.user_title: Build Firebase-powered applications with AI assistance
content.value_statement: Firebase development requires complex setup and ongoing maintenance. This skill provides authoritative guidance for AI agents to manage Firebase projects, execute CLI commands, and implement best practices correctly.
actual_capabilities: Guide complete Firebase environment setup including Node.js and Firebase CLI installation
Execute Firebase CLI commands using npx for latest tooling
Configure Firebase MCP server integration for AI agents
Manage Firebase authentication and project context
Provide SDK setup guidance for web applications
Update and refresh agent skills and plugin configurations
limitations: Requires user to manually complete browser-based authentication flows
Cannot create Firebase projects without user-provided project IDs
Agent-specific setup requires identifying the correct environment (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.)
Some setup steps require user confirmation before proceeding

use_cases.title: New Firebase Project Setup
use_cases.description: Guides developers through complete Firebase initialization from Node.js installation through project creation and SDK configuration.

use_cases.title: AI Agent Firebase Integration
use_cases.description: Configures AI coding assistants with Firebase MCP server and agent skills for seamless Firebase development workflows.

use_cases.title: Environment Maintenance
use_cases.description: Keeps Firebase tooling and agent skills updated with refresh procedures for various AI agent environments.

prompt_templates.title: Check Firebase Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify my Firebase development environment is properly configured. Check Node.js version, Firebase CLI installation, and authentication status.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize Firebase for Web App
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Firebase for a new web application. I need to create a project, register a web app, and configure the SDK with proper initialization.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Firebase MCP Server
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up the Firebase MCP server for my AI agent environment. I use Claude Code and need both the agent skills and MCP configuration installed.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Firebase Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: My Firebase CLI login is not working. The browser will not open during authentication. Help me authenticate using an alternative method.

output_examples.input: Check if Firebase CLI is installed
output_examples.output: Run 'npx -y firebase-tools@latest --version' to verify installation. If successful, you'll see a version number. If the command fails, the Firebase CLI needs to be installed.

output_examples.input: Initialize Firebase services for a new project
output_examples.output: Execute Firebase init command to select and configure services. The CLI will prompt for service selection (Firestore, Authentication, Functions, etc.) and create corresponding configuration files in your project directory.

best_practices: Always use npx -y firebase-tools@latest instead of global firebase installation to ensure latest version
Prioritize official Firebase documentation and knowledge sources over general search results
Use Firebase MCP server tools for remote API interactions instead of manual API calls
anti_patterns: Using 'firebase' command directly without npx wrapper - may use outdated global installation
Skipping authentication verification before executing project commands
Attempting Firebase operations without active project context

faq.question: Why use npx instead of installing Firebase CLI globally?
faq.answer: Using npx ensures you always run the latest Firebase CLI version without manual updates. Global installations can become outdated and cause compatibility issues.

faq.question: How do I authenticate Firebase CLI in a headless environment?
faq.answer: Use 'npx -y firebase-tools@latest login --no-localhost' for environments without browser access. This provides a code-based authentication flow.

faq.question: What is the Firebase MCP server?
faq.answer: The Firebase MCP server provides tools for AI agents to interact with Firebase APIs directly, enabling automated operations like fetching Crashlytics logs or executing Data Connect queries.

faq.question: How do I check which Firebase project is active?
faq.answer: Run 'npx -y firebase-tools@latest use' to display the active project. If no project is active, you will need to link one using the use command with a project ID.

faq.question: Can this skill create Firebase projects automatically?
faq.answer: No. Project creation requires user interaction and decisions. The skill can guide through the process but will prompt for user confirmation and project ID input.

faq.question: How often should I update my Firebase agent skills?
faq.answer: Check for updates regularly, especially when encountering issues with Firebase commands. Firebase best practices evolve quickly, and outdated skills may use deprecated workflows.
